title: scurrula thelocarpa
intro: Scurrula thelocarpa, also known as poison tree, is a plant species within the family Santalaceae, native to tropical Africa and Asia. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which can be used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits associated with Scurrula thelocarpa include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and treat skin infections. Herbal preparations of Scurrula thelocarpa can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine to promote overall well-being.
